understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting every selectable piece onto goal tiles marked with a '+'. If there are multiple '+' tiles, they must all be occupied by selectable pieces at the same time for the level to report WIN, and completing all levels completes the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: At any time one piece is the currently selected piece (the one that moves). MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN attempts to move the selected piece in that direction, but movement is constrained by walls/obstacles and/or other blocking tiles (so the move can result in no position change if blocked). CLICK acts as a directed move/relocation command for the selected piece: if the clicked target square is reachable under the movement rules (i.e., not blocked by intervening walls/obstacles), the selected piece moves to that clicked position; otherwise the piece does not move. Several clicks had no effect, consistent with blocked paths or invalid targets.

orient_history: Arrow-key movement is blocked by adjacent walls, so progress likely requires mouse interaction: clicking a goal position may place/teleport the currently selected piece onto that goal region (rather than moving through empty cells).

CLICK likely changes which selectable is active (selection), and/or attempts to place/teleport the selected piece; since movement is fully walled in, next I will verify selection switching by clicking the other selectable.

CLICK appears to change which selectable is controlled; MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) but is blocked by collidable wall tiles. Next step is to select the other piece to see if it has available moves toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a selectable piece under the cursor (switching the 'player'/selected piece), while MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) but is blocked by walls. To make progress, we should switch to the other selectable and see if it has different mobility toward the goal.

CLICK seems to primarily change which selectable is active (earlier 'moved' logs likely reflected switching control between identical pieces), while MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece one cell but may fail if enclosed by walls. Need to probe whether MOVE_* has any special behavior despite 'blocked' flags.
Hypothesis: CLICKing directly on another selectable changes which piece is selected (as opposed to moving), allowing us to control the other piece that may have mobility toward the goal area.

CLICK likely either (a) selects a different selectable when clicking on it, or (b) relocates the selected piece to the clicked tile if valid. Since movement is fully blocked, we need to change which piece is selected and test if the other piece has a path toward the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* attempts a 1-cell (3px) step and is blocked by adjacent collidable walls; CLICK attempts to reposition the selected piece toward/at the clicked coordinate (possibly snapping to nearest valid cell), and can move it out of a locally trapped spot even when MOVE_* is blocked.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to move/teleport the selected piece to the clicked tile/anchor if it is a valid destination; precise pixel coordinate may need to match the goal object's anchor (top-left) rather than an arbitrary point inside the '+' region. Since normal moves are fully blocked, progress likely requires a successful CLICK relocation.

Clicks seem to cause a teleport/jump of the currently selected piece to certain valid anchor cells (possibly the cell clicked or a linked node), even when normal MOVE_* is blocked. To make progress, we likely need to CLICK back to a less-trapped anchor ([9,21]) and then try different clicks/moves to reach the goal area.

New hypothesis (based only on observed outcomes): CLICK seems to cause a jump/transfer between compartments or predefined snap points, while MOVE_* performs normal grid movement when possible. If a piece is immobile, MOVE_* may still be used to change which selectable is active (cycle selection) or otherwise interact with the state even when blocked.

Clicks likely change selection to the selectable under/near the clicked coordinates (the log reports this as the 'selected piece moved'). MOVE_* moves only the currently selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) but is blocked by walls; so to make progress we should switch to the other selectable and see if it has a different set of available moves.

CLICK likely either (a) selects a different selectable when clicking on it, or (b) attempts to reposition the currently-selected piece to a clicked/snapped location if valid. Since the current selected piece is fully boxed-in by walls, progress may require switching selection to the other selectable and moving that one toward the goal.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is active (and/or snaps the active piece to another selectable’s position). Since MOVE is fully blocked for the currently selected piece, we should switch selection to the other selectable and re-check its possible moves toward the goal.

CLICK seems to change which selectable is active when clicking on/near the other selectable; MOVE_* only moves the currently selected piece but can be blocked by walls. To make progress toward the enclosed goal region, I likely need to operate with the other selectable first.

CLICK seems to switch which selectable is controlled (and the log reports it as the 'selected piece moved' to the clicked selectable’s position). If one piece is stuck, I should switch to the other to see if it has a path/teleport interaction toward the goal.

New hypothesis based only on observations: CLICK is primarily selecting between the two identical selectables (making it look like the ‘selected piece moved’), while MOVE_* attempts to translate the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) unless blocked by walls. The currently selected piece appears fully enclosed, so we likely need to operate the other selectable to reach the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* attempts a 1-cell step and respects collisions; CLICK attempts a slide/teleport toward the clicked x/y (often along the same row/column as the piece), stopping at the furthest reachable free spot before a wall/obstacle. To reach the goal area, we likely need to use CLICK to reposition since normal movement is fully blocked.

CLICK likely either (a) selects the piece you click on, or (b) attempts to move the currently selected piece toward the clicked location; since movement is fully blocked for the current piece, switching selection to the other selectable may give a movable piece or change how CLICK-moves behave.

CLICK likely changes the controlled/active piece or teleports the selected piece to the clicked location/region when valid (previous CLICKs caused large position changes despite walls). Since MOVE is fully blocked, progress probably requires CLICKing onto/into the goal region to reposition a selectable there.

MOVE_* attempts to shift the currently-selected 1-cell piece by 3 pixels (1 tile) unless blocked; CLICK can change which selectable is active when clicking on/near a selectable (and may also attempt a snap-move/teleport when clicking elsewhere, but that has been inconsistent).

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable switches selection to that piece; CLICK on empty space attempts to slide the currently selected piece toward the click until blocked by walls. Since the current piece is fully boxed in, selecting the other piece may allow movement.

New hypothesis (discarding prior click-to-teleport idea): CLICK likely just switches/sets a target between the two selectables when reachable, but actual progress requires sliding the selected 3x3 piece through open corridors using MOVE actions; the reason CLICKing the goal did nothing is that pathing/interaction is blocked by walls, so I must reposition via moves (or confirm I am stuck in a pocket).

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a selectable if the click hits it; otherwise CLICK has no effect. MOVE_* moves the currently selected selectable by one cell (3 pixels) but is blocked by walls/other selectables. To make progress, I must select the other piece and try moving it to create space / reach the goal area.

Hypothesis: CLICK moves the selected piece to the nearest 3-pixel grid-aligned empty position derived from the click (e.g., 13→12, 22→21). Clicking directly on the goal object may not trigger movement; instead I should click a grid-aligned coordinate at the top-left of a goal cell area (likely outside the goal sprite’s exact bounding box) so the move occurs and the piece overlaps the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK may interact with tiles (not just move); specifically, clicking a wall tile might toggle/remove it or otherwise change the layout. Since movement is fully blocked, the next progress attempt is to CLICK the adjacent wall directly to test if walls can be altered.

CLICK likely attempts to reposition the selected piece to the clicked cell (teleport-like), but only succeeds if the target lands on a valid empty/goal cell coordinate (possibly requiring 3-pixel alignment) and not inside wall collision. The earlier goal clicks may have failed due to misalignment with the actual goal cells.

Hypothesis: CLICKing on a selectable may switch which piece is selected (even if the log sometimes reports it as a move). Since the currently selected piece cannot MOVE, we likely must change selection to the other piece to make progress.

New hypothesis based only on observation: CLICK can relocate/select pieces but cannot bypass walls/blocked adjacency; to make progress I likely need to resolve the immediate blockage by interacting via movement (possibly pushing/swapping with the adjacent selectable), since my selected piece is boxed in.

CLICKing on empty space attempts to move the currently selected piece to the clicked position if a valid path/region allows it; CLICKing on a selectable likely switches selection (or moves onto it). Since the current piece cannot move, we must switch to the other selectable and try to move it onto the goal.

CLICK appears to reposition the selected piece to a chosen coordinate when that placement is valid (not colliding). Since normal moves are blocked, the only chance to win is a final CLICK directly onto a goal-covered coordinate that fits the piece.

Actions consume steps; with 0 steps remaining, movement/teleport via CLICK likely cannot complete the objective. If the engine still accepts an input, the only remaining chance is that a CLICK could trigger a non-movement state change (e.g., reselect or win-check), but based on logs, CLICK normally consumes steps and moves/attempts to move the currently selected piece.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by getting every selectable piece onto goal tiles marked with a '+'. If there are multiple '+' tiles, they must all be occupied by selectable pieces at the same time for the level to report WIN, and completing all levels completes the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: At any time one piece is the currently selected piece (the one that moves). MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN attempts to move the selected piece in that direction, but movement is constrained by walls/obstacles and/or other blocking tiles (so the move can result in no position change if blocked). CLICK acts as a directed move/relocation command for the selected piece: if the clicked target square is reachable under the movement rules (i.e., not blocked by intervening walls/obstacles), the selected piece moves to that clicked position; otherwise the piece does not move. Several clicks had no effect, consistent with blocked paths or invalid targets.
